author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
e mana! vicāri kena nā cāo
dekha bhava-roga, te kene ghuce nā,
kata nā auṣadha khāo

kata nā karicha, prasāda bhakṣaṇa,
caraṇa⋅dhauta jala
e saba auṣadhi, pāna kara tabu,
dhātute nāhika bala

jihvāra paraśe, je hari-nāmete,
premete bhāsāya tanu
se nāma loiye, ārdra nā hoili,
lohāra piṇḍa se janu

bhāviyā dekha nā, auṣadhe ki kare,
kupathya chāḍite nāro
kupathya thākile, roga nā chāḍibe,
aruci bāḍibe āro

anupāna jāni, auṣadhi khāo to,
rogera damana habe
ekhano tā jadi, bujhite nā pāra,
tabe se bujhibe kabe

kṣudhāṭī bāḍaye, ruciṭī janame,
khāite ānanda⋅jala
kahe premānanda, tabe se jāniha,
auṣadhi⋅dhāraṇa⋅phala
en_translation:
O my mind! Why do you not think and see for yourself? Behold the disease of material existence—why does it not disappear, even though you take so many "medicines"?

How much food offered to the Lord do you partake, and how much water that has washed His feet? You drink all these medicines, yet there is no spiritual strength in your constitution.

The Holy Name of Lord Hari, by its very touch upon the tongue, can float the entire body in divine love. Yet taking that Name, your heart has not become softened; such a person is like a lump of iron.

Just think—what can medicine do if you cannot give up unwholesome food? If you continue to eat such things, the disease will not leave you, and your aversion to spiritual life will only increase.

If you take the medicine with the correct knowledge and proper following of instructions, the disease will be suppressed. If you still cannot understand this, then when will you ever understand?

Only when spiritual hunger increases and a taste for divine bliss awakens should you know that the medicine is truly taking effect. Premānanda says: "Only then will you know the true fruit of the medicine."
